title: Natural products from the human microbiota - Discovery Results
key_discoveries: Identification of extensive BGC repertoire in human microbiota
Discovery of diverse microbiota-derived bioactive metabolites
Characterization of small molecule mediators of microbe-host interactions
Novel insights into microbiota ecological roles

microbiota_bgc_landscape.scope: Numerous BGCs identified in genomes of human-associated bacteria
microbiota_bgc_landscape.diversity: Multiple classes of biosynthetic pathways (PKS, NRPS, RiPP, terpenes)
microbiota_bgc_landscape.significance: Human microbiota has significant biosynthetic potential

ripp_ecological_roles.antiphage_defense: Small peptides with ≤50 amino acids may act as antiphage defense

symbiosis_insights: Mammals potentially part of symbiotic paradigm
Microbiota metabolites affect host physiology and health
computational_analysis_findings: Genome mining reveals vast biosynthetic potential previously unknown
BGC algorithms have automated BGC identification
Unexpected discovery of numerous functional clusters
research_implications: Human microbiota is source of novel bioactive compounds
Small molecule mediators critical for mutualism
Dysbiosis may involve loss of key metabolite producers
future_research_directions: Systematic characterization of all human microbiota BGCs
Identification of additional microbe-derived bioactive molecules
Development of therapeutics based on microbiota metabolites
Restoration of metabolite-producing capacity in dysbiotic individuals
